item.title: The Best CBD Products, According to Cool People Including tinctures, topicals, edibles, and more. - New York Magazine
These Still-Cool Stan Smiths Are 40 Percent Off And they’re available in three different colors. - New York Magazine
12 Things on Sale You’ll Want: From West Elm to Shopbop Including a bunch of wardrobe essentials that’ll transition nicely between seasons. - New York Magazine
What the Glazed NYC Designers Can’t Live Without “It has a strong, intense taste, but I just imagine I’m eating pizza whenever I take it.” - New York Magazine
I Want to Cover My Entire Desk in These TikTok Light Strips From YouTube to Twitch to TikTok, light strips are everywhere. - New York Magazine
The 7 Types of Cocktail Glasses You Need at Home A V-shaped martini glass is not one of them. - New York Magazine
6 Things That’ll Definitely Sell Out: From Adidas to Away For starters, new colors of time-tested favorites. - New York Magazine
The Best Android Tablets, According to Experts Including one for only $99. - New York Magazine

What Are the Best (Actually Nice-Looking) Pill Cases? Including a “sleek” pill-case-slash-compact and objet d’art pill boxes from Jonathan Adler and Judith Leiber, all of which you’ll want to display. - New York Magazine
This Portable Anker Speaker Is the Cheapest We’ve Seen It It’s on our list of the best things to buy from the Strategist-approved tech brand. - New York Magazine
How I’d Redo My Baby Registry: With a Folding Bassinet I’m six-foot-seven, so this made it easier for me to bend down and pick him up. - New York Magazine
10 Things on Sale You’ll Want: From Nike to Mansur Gavriel We’re antsy for springtime, and so are the sales. - New York Magazine

The Best Body Lotions for Dry Skin, According to Experts Including everyday options; lotions for dry, sensitive skins; and specific products to treat inflamed dry skin and dryness caused by eczema. - New York Magazine
The Best Down Pillows, According to Experts Whether you sleep on your side, stomach, back, or all three, the right down pillow can bring a touch of hotel-style luxury to your bed. - New York Magazine
These Small Drawstring Trash Bags Are a Revelation “As the saying (now) goes, one man’s trash (bag) is another man’s treasure.” - New York Magazine
5 Serums Under $20 That Work Just As Well As Expensive Ones Serums don’t need to be expensive to order to be effective. - New York Magazine
The Best Subscription Boxes for Every Type of Person It’s the gift that literally keeps on giving. - New York Magazine
The Best Mascaras, According to Makeup Artists Including lengthening, volumizing, and waterproof options. - New York Magazine
9 Reasons to Believe the Worst of the Pandemic Is Over The good news is really starting to roll in. (Though there are also a few reasons for caution.) - New York Magazine

Trump’s Reconquest of the GOP Has an Upside for Democracy Averting minority rule requires radicalizing Senate Democrats. The GOP renewing its vows of fealty to Trump facilitates that task. - New York Magazine

The IRS Could Eat the Recently Unemployed’s $1,400 Checks If Biden doesn’t forgive the taxes on millions of Americans’ unemployment benefits, they might not forgive him for their giant surprise tax bills. - New York Magazine
Scandal-Plagued McKinsey Ousts Leader It is telling that partners at the elite and secretive consulting firm have chosen not to give Kevin Sneader a second term at the helm. - New York Magazine
Why Did First Look Let Go of Laura Poitras? A co-founder turns critic. - New York Magazine

Fanne Fox, Wilbur Mills, and a Tale of Washington Hubris The woman involved in the implosion of one of Washington’s most bloated careers has died, and with her a golden era of D.C. sex scandals. - New York Magazine
Why Did Someone Pay $5 Million for a Baseball Card in 2021? The pandemic isn’t the only reason the sports-card market has been booming lately. - New York Magazine
The Republican War to Roll Back the New Deal Is Finally Over A decades-long quest quietly perishes. - New York Magazine
De Blasio Calls for Probe Into Cuomo Sexual Harassment Claim The mayor called the allegations “disturbing” and joined a bipartisan group of New York lawmakers demanding a probe. - New York Magazine
GOP Senators Haven’t Represented a Majority Since 1996 The anti-majoritarian nature of the Senate has helped Republicans block progress for many years. - New York Magazine
Oatly Is Going Public Five years after taking over every fancy coffee shop, the oat-milk company could be worth billions on the Stock Exchange. - New York Magazine

Key Moments from the Senate’s First Hearing on the Capitol Riot Security officials who were in charge on January 6 claimed in Senate testimony that they had insufficient intelligence on the potential threat. - New York Magazine
Bernie Sanders Casts First Democratic Vote Against a Biden Cabinet Nominee Tom Vilsack was overwhelmingly confirmed anyway, and Democratic unity remains impressive. - New York Magazine

Rochester Police Officers Will Not Be Charged in Killing of Daniel Prude A grand jury determined that the officers who put Prude in the chokehold that led to his death last March will not face charges. - New York Magazine

How Is Ted Cruz Trying to Defend His Cancún Vacation Now? The senator has blamed his daughters, the media, and his neighbors for his decision to ditch Texas for warm weather during last week’s freeze. - New York Magazine

Former NYPD Officer Charged With Assaulting an Officer at the Capitol Riot Thomas Webster, who reportedly once guarded City Hall, has been accused of beating a D.C. officer with a flagpole at the insurrection. - New York Magazine
Pence Not Holding Grudge Against Trump Over Whole Riot Thing The man who defined vice presidential loyalty couldn’t stay mad for long. - New York Magazine

Agonized Georgia Republicans Long for Herschel Walker to Run for Senate A battered and deeply divided state party bent on suppressing Black votes could use a Trump-friendly Black sports legend. - New York Magazine

South Dakota Attorney General Reported Hitting a Deer But Fatally Struck a Man New details cast doubt on Jason Ravnsborg’s initial claim that he thought he hit a deer, not a person. He is now facing impeachment. - New York Magazine

Former Aide Accuses Governor Cuomo of Sexual Harassment Lindsey Boylan says she endured years of sexual harassment from Cuomo, and resigned in 2018 after he kissed her in a meeting without her consent. - New York Magazine

Everything We Know About the Coronavirus Variant Spreading in New York City Two new studies suggest that the variant B.1.526 detected in New York City could dampen the effectiveness of some vaccine candidates. - New York Magazine

Republicans Return to Old Voter-Suppression Playbook Trump shifted the focus to phony vote-by-mail fraud claims, but Republicans tried to limit all kinds of voting pre-2020 — and they’re at it again. - New York Magazine
Tanden’s Nomination Won’t Make or Break the Biden Presidency Some Beltway pundits are inflating this story well beyond its significance. - New York Magazine

Mitch McConnell Would ‘Absolutely’ Support Trump If He Were the 2024 Nominee Once again, he backs the man who has physically mocked him, called on the party to replace him, and arguably cost him his Senate majority. - New York Magazine
